summary: Install-time fetch from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/index.js line 14 issues a fetch() to https://pub-0dc3e1677e894f07bbea11b17a29e032.r2.dev, an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket of the same shape used by prior known-malicious npm clusters to drop platform-native binaries. The call is parameterized with process.platform and process.versions, indicating platform-targeted payload selection — the canonical dropper fingerprint (host-fingerprint + opaque host-controlled bytes). The destination is not version-pinned, not on the publisher's own infrastructure, has no hash/signature verification, and the bucket contents are mutable by whoever holds the R2 credentials. Anonymous R2 buckets at install or load time are payload-distribution infrastructure; legitimate native-addon downloads use the publisher's GitHub releases at a pinned version matching the package version. Installing or loading this package retrieves and uses attacker-mutable bytes selected for the installer's OS and Node version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
